data.text/markdown:
The Dead Salmons of AI Interpretability

In 2009, researcher Craig Bennett placed a dead Atlantic salmon in an fMRI machine and showed it pictures of humans in social situations. When analyzing the data without correcting for multiple comparisons, the fMRI detected "brain activity" in the dead fish. If your math lacks sanity checks, it will hallucinate intelligence out of pure noise.
Your Challenge, Judge: Tweak the parameters below to force our untrained, "dead fish" AI to show statistically significant intelligence.
